title: Pope's first day in Algeria marred by failed suicide attack near Algiers
subhed: Two men blew themselves up on Monday in downtown Blida, about 40 kilometers south of the Algerian capital, without causing any casualties other than themselves. Leo XIV had arrived just hours earlier in Algiers for the first visit by a pope to the country.

title: France and UK to co-host Strait of Hormuz talks Friday
subhed: President Emmanuel Macron and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer will co-chair the meeting to discuss a plan to 'restore freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz when security conditions allow.'

title: Low turnout could shape the 2027 French presidential race
subhed: Abstainers from the 2026 municipal elections could fuel a protest vote in the presidential election, writes Anne Muxel, emeritus research professor at CEVIPOF, based on the French electoral survey by Ipsos BVA-CESI Ecole d'Ingénieurs for Le Monde, CEVIPOF and the Jean Jaurès Foundation.

title: 'Natural bread, more digestible and free of toxic substances, could be available to all if we changed the rules'
subhed: Paul Magnette, president of the French-speaking Socialist Party of Belgium, addresses concerns about bread consumption – a symbol of French culture – following a report on the presence of the heavy metal cadmium in certain cultivated plants, including wheat.

Lebanese mourn victims of 'Black Wednesday': 'We are not just numbers'
subhed: On April 9 in Qmatiyeh, near Beirut, the family of Fatima Amhaz buried the 33-year-old pharmacist who was killed by an Israeli airstrike the previous day. That same day, at least 357 people lost their lives in Lebanon, marking the deadliest day recorded since the war between Israel and Hezbollah began on March 2.

title: In Algeria, the pope follows in the footsteps of the country's Christian history and reaches out to Muslims
subhed: On Monday, Leo XIV will become the first pontiff in history to visit Algeria, birthplace of the renowned Christian philosopher Saint Augustine. It was in Hippo, now Annaba, that the theologian wrote his most significant works in the fourth century.

title: Pope embarks on African tour, a strategic continent for Catholicism
subhed: From April 13 to 23, Pope Léon XIV is set to visit Algeria, Cameroon, Angola and Equatorial Guinea. The trip symbolizes the shift in the center of gravity of the Catholic Church, whose future now lies in Africa.
